description: Streamlit is an open-source Python framework that makes it easy to build and share beautiful, custom web apps for machine learning and data science. With Streamlit, you can turn data scripts into shareable web applications in minutes without needing front-end experience.

Developed a machine learning framework to predict peak electricity demand at daily and hourly levels using load and weather data. Built Random Forest models to identify peak periods, handled class imbalance, and evaluated performance with ROC-AUC and F1-score. Deployed via a Streamlit dashboard for forecasting and grid support.

Aspiring AI Engineer specializing in Machine Learning, Data Science, and Generative AI. Passionate about building scalable, real-world AI applications using Python, ML frameworks, and modern AI tools.

A curated collection of Machine Learning projects developed during my learning journey. Features end-to-end ML pipelines, exploratory data analysis (EDA), and various algorithms implemented using Python and popular ML frameworks.
A productionised PINNS machine learning framework for modeling vortex-induced vibrations in fluid-structure interaction problems using physics-informed neural networks.

An advanced predictive framework designed to forecast landslide susceptibility and displacement. By integrating machine learning with real-time geographical and meteorological data, this project aims to provide early-warning systems to save lives and infrastructure in high-risk zones.

This project develops a bias-aware machine learning framework for hypertension biomarker discovery in underrepresented populations. Using six integrated RNA-seq datasets, the study applies ancestry stratification, batch-effect correction, and explainable AI (XAI) to identify and validate ancestry-specific biomarkers.

MiniML is a lightweight Machine and Deep Learning framework dedicated to training neural networks in embedded systems, redefining conventional and heavyweight models into different methods that can be exported and executed on low-cost microcontrollers (Arduino, ESP32, STM32).

A research-driven lightweight Edge-AI framework for early cardiac risk prediction from ECG signals. This project focuses on ECG preprocessing, interpretable feature extraction, and efficient machine learning models for accurate and real-time cardiovascular risk assessment on edge devices.

A clean, object-oriented implementation of Linear Regression built from scratch in Python using NumPy. Features a vectorized Gradient Descent optimization loop capable of handling both simple and multiple linear regression without relying on high-level machine learning frameworks
